channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Pronto expands online domestic services in India with rapid growth and $100M valuation
item.description: Bengaluru-based startup Pronto is rapidly transforming India's largely informal domestic services market by moving it online. Founded less than a year ago, Pronto recently raised $25 million in a Seri...

item.title: Alberta's Mismanagement of Oil Wealth Threatens Future Generations
item.description: Alberta’s oil wealth has long been a source of provincial pride, but its management has raised concerns over the years. Despite efforts like the Heritage Fund, established in 1976 to save for future g...

item.title: Johannesburg’s water supply issues persist despite political interventions
item.description: Johannesburg is facing serious water supply challenges, with residents across different suburbs experiencing inconsistent access. In Melville, community protests forced Joburg Water to restore supply ...

item.title: LTFRB may approve temporary fare hikes amid rising diesel prices
item.description: The Land Transportation Franchising and Regulatory Board (LTFRB) is reviewing requests for provisional fare increases for public transport operators if diesel prices reach P60 per liter. The move come...

item.title: FM Dar expresses worry over US-Israel attack on Iran despite recent diplomatic progress
item.description: Pakistan's Foreign Minister Ishaq Dar expressed concern over the sudden US-Israel coordinated attack on Iran, despite recent progress in diplomatic talks between Washington and Tehran. Addressing the ...

item.title: PSG Increase Lead to Four Points After Narrow Win Over Le Havre
item.description: Paris Saint-Germain (PSG) stretched their lead at the top of Ligue 1 to four points following a hard-fought 1-0 victory against Le Havre. With second-placed Lens drawing against Strasbourg, PSG capita...

item.title: UNSC Panel Warns on Growing Threat from Banned TTP to Pakistan
item.description: The United Nations Security Council’s Daesh and Al-Qaeda Sanctions Committee, chaired by Denmark, has highlighted that the banned Tehreek-e-Taliban Pakistan (TTP) continues to pose a serious threat to...

item.title: Supreme Court temporarily allows California schools to notify parents about students’ gender transitions
item.description: On March 2, 2026, the U.S. Supreme Court issued a ruling allowing California schools to inform parents if their children wish to change their gender identity, temporarily blocking a state law that pro...

item.title: UK Military Operations in the Middle East Amid Rising Tensions with Iran
item.description: The British government has confirmed that the RAF base at Akrotiri, Cyprus, was struck in a drone attack amid escalating tensions in the Middle East. The resumption of US and Israeli air strikes on Ir...

item.title: Depopulation Crisis in Uttarakhand’s Remote Villages
item.description: In Uttarakhand’s remote hill regions, particularly in the Pauri district, villages are witnessing extreme depopulation as residents migrate to urban areas for better opportunities. Baingwadi, a small ...

item.title: BuCor launches probe into preferential treatment allegations at Sablayan Prison
item.description: Bureau of Corrections (BuCor) Director General Gregorio Catapang Jr. has ordered an investigation into the allegations that some Chinese detainees at the Sablayan Prison and Penal Farm (SPPF) in Occid...

item.title: Muhimbili Orthopaedic Institute Clarifies Amputation Cases Among Bodaboda Riders
item.description: The Muhimbili Orthopaedic Institute (MOI) has responded to misconceptions about its practices involving bodaboda (motorcycle taxi) accident victims. Dr. Mpoki Ulisubisya, the MOI director, explained t...

item.title: NAPS Partners with SmartPay Finance to Launch Paytogeda Initiative for Polytechnic Students' Financial Inclusion
item.description: The Senate leadership of the National Association of Polytechnic Students (NAPS) has entered into a partnership with SmartPay Finance and Holdings Limited to introduce the Paytogeda Initiative, a fint...

item.title: US Deputy Secretary Christopher Landau to discuss trade and Indo-Pacific cooperation during India visit
item.description: United States Deputy Secretary of State Christopher Landau is set to visit India to lead the US delegation at the Raisina Dialogue 2026 in New Delhi. The annual geopolitical conference is organised by...

item.title: British Tourists in Doha and Dubai Stranded Amid Missile Strikes and Evacuations
item.description: As the conflict in the Middle East escalates, British tourists stranded in Doha and Dubai have shared harrowing accounts of missile strikes and chaotic evacuations. Over 300,000 Britons are believed t...

item.title: Guidelines for Resigning Professionally and Handling Counteroffers
item.description: Resigning from a job can be an emotional and awkward process, as it involves stepping away from relationships, routines, and a familiar way of life. Shane Little, Managing Director at Hays Asia Pacifi...

item.title: Davido Facing ₦1B Lawsuit Over Defamation and Cyberbullying Allegations
item.description: Davido is currently facing a ₦1 billion lawsuit filed by Sophie Momodu’s lawyer, Mrs. Ebelechukwu Egeonu Enedah. The suit follows allegations of defamation, cyberbullying, and threats to life made aga...

item.title: House panel removes offensive comment by Rep. Bong Suntay about Anne Curtis during impeachment hearing
item.description: At a House Justice Committee hearing on the impeachment complaints against Vice President Sara Duterte, Representative Bong Suntay faced backlash for making an inappropriate comment about actress Anne...

item.title: Rinsola Babajide hails WAFCON triumph as career highlight
item.description: Omorinsola Rinsola Babajide don show say football don carry her waka far, but nothing wey she don achieve reach like to lift the Women’s Africa Cup of Nations (WAFCON) trophy with Nigeria Super Falcon...

item.title: Durban mother reunites with her baby after using a baby saver
item.description: A young mother in Durban, who initially placed her newborn in a baby saver at Isiaiah 54 Children’s Sanctuary, has been reunited with her child after changing her mind. The mother returned to the sanc...
